title: Genomic DNA Extraction Protocol
author: Michael Burgis
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bwx3pfqn

steps.title: TEN Buffer (10 mM Tris-HCl, 10...
steps.instruction: TEN Buffer (10 mM Tris-HCl, 10 mM EDTA, 150 mM NaCl) SDS-EB Buffer (2% SDS, 400 mM NaCl, 40 mM EDTA, 100 mM Tris-HCl, pH 8.0) TE Buffer (10mM Tris-HCl pH 8.0, 1mM EDTA pH 8.0) [autoclaved]

steps.title: Pellet the 10ml overnight culture of...
steps.instruction: Pellet the 10ml overnight culture of your algae or cyanobacteria in a 15ml falcon at 5.000g.

steps.title: Resuspend in 5ml of TEN Buffer...
steps.instruction: Resuspend in 5ml of TEN Buffer by vortexing, spin for 10 sec. and aspirate off supernatant.

steps.title: Resuspend cells in 150 ul H2O...
steps.instruction: Resuspend cells in 150 ul H2O on ice and add 300 ul of SDS-EB buffer, vortex to mix.

steps.title: Extract once with 3
steps.instruction: Extract once with 3.5 ml phenol/Chloroform:isoamyl alcohol(1:1) for a few min by vortexing, separate phases by centrifugation for 5 min., transfer aq. phase to a new falcon tube.

steps.title: Extract once with 3 ml Chloroform:isoamyl alcohol (24:1), transfer aq
steps.instruction: Extract once with 3 ml Chloroform:isoamyl alcohol (24:1), transfer aq. to a new tube.

steps.title: Repeat step in order to maximize purity
steps.instruction: Repeat step in order to maximize purity.

steps.title: Add 2 volumes abs
steps.instruction: Add 2 volumes abs. ethanol, incubate on ice for 30 min., centrifuge for 10 min., wash pellet once with 200 ul 70% ethanol.

steps.title: Dry at 60°C until all ethanol...
steps.instruction: Dry at 60°C until all ethanol evaporated and resuspend in desired volume of TE Buffer.

steps.title: Incubate at 50 °C for 1...
steps.instruction: Incubate at 50 °C for 1 to 2 h to ensure complete re-suspension. (Can be extended to overnight incubation)

steps.title: Measure the quality and quantity of...
steps.instruction: Measure the quality and quantity of your DNA using a NanoDrop.
